title: Hikaris sisters
wikitext:
[]: Hikari's sisters?             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Sep 22, 2014 5:17 am So as we all know, Hikari has 2 sisters- Nozomi and Kodama, named after Shinkansen lines. However, they never actually appear in the series; they are referred to, but we never even see them. Is there any "official" art of the other 2 Horaki sisters? The only visual renditions I've seen of them have been Meguro Fuzuko's art...and the less said about that, the better. Did Sadamoto do illustrations for them? Did they appear in any "official" spin-offs?

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Sep 22, 2014 2:18 pm I think we once saw Nozomi in Angelic days, in a single panel, during the final battle. (The sisters huddle together while everyone else prepares to fight) She basically looked like a younger clone of Hikari with slightly shorter pigtails.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Mon Sep 22, 2014 4:11 pm I can't corroborate, since I've only read the first volume of Angelic Days (and several years ago, at that!) Otherwise, though, I can't think of when I'd have seen 'em. They're not in NGE, NTE, Sadamoto's Manga, or even the parody manga that Dark Horse published.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Mon Sep 22, 2014 4:16 pm I quickly reviewed Angelic Days and haven't seen Nozomi anywhere during the final battle. Could've missed her, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Sep 22, 2014 4:17 pm I'll always just think of Meguro's designs for the characters. (I mean, as designs, they were perfectly good. It's just what he did with them that I'll be attempting to expunge from my mind.)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Sep 23, 2014 12:22 am Let's not have that tangent, okay? Goggle if you must, but it's likely to be NSFW... or anywhere else, for that matter.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Sep 23, 2014 2:59 am IIRC, Kodama is also mentioned in ANIMA: she died during an attack of the new bad guys, which incited Hikari to accept an offer from Europe to become an Eva pilot to get revenge. (she even later saves Shinji's life in extremis!)

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Sep 23, 2014 3:51 pm Might've been some other battle; I'm too lazy to search for it now, but it's not like Angelic Days had so many battles in it... In any case, we can extract a little from what we see in the series, at least about Kodama: The weird student/date guy from episode 15 was a friend of hers, and even though she's the eldest, Hikari took up the replacement mom role, perhaps hinting that Kodama doesn't have a personality compatible with being the "reasonable big sibling". Maybe she's a bit of a rebellious teen, or simply a bit clumsy.

[]:             Quote Post by Squigsquasher » Mon Sep 29, 2014 7:22 pm @Reichu: I'm inclined to agree. The designs themselves were actually pretty good, but as you say, what went on with them...yeurch. Meguro is a really talented artist; it's a shame he/she draws so much fucked up shit. Meguro's designs are usually my go-to depiction of them (basically an older and a younger Hikari, both with different pigtails) and I'd be happy using them as references for fics/art (IIRC, he/she/it actually did a reference type group shot picture, starring Shiro Tokita of all people and the derpiest Asuka ever). I just wondered if there was any official art of them. @Kendrix: Hmm, that's interesting. It's amazing how much you can discover about a character who doesn't even appear in the series- in any case, I'd like to see them expanded upon.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Sep 30, 2014 2:55 am @ Kendrix: It could also be that Kodama works to keep the household afloat and simply doesn't have time/is too tired when back at home to take care of the household. By the way, did we ever had any info on the father of the Horaki sisters? Also I can't believe none of us brought up yet that our dear member JimmyWolks also made a rendition of Kodama! (she works in the kindergarten where Shinji and Asuka chose for Aki!)

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 30, 2014 11:23 am ElMariachi wrote: @ Kendrix: It could also be that Kodama works to keep the household afloat and simply doesn't have time/is too tired when back at home to take care of the household. Byt the way, did we ever had any info on the father of the Horaki sisters?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Sep 30, 2014 12:07 pm Sachi wrote: My head canon has the father being the one that works hard to keep the family afloat (possibly as a Nerv techie) and is therefore too tired to take care of the household affairs, while the sister was perhaps as Kendrix said; or perhaps even the elder sister is in college?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Sep 30, 2014 12:58 pm Bagheera wrote: Given that Asuka's date was a medical student this seems likely. I'm still creeped out by the notion of a med student going on a date with a 13-year-old.

[]:             Quote Post by amitakartok » Tue Sep 30, 2014 1:23 pm Or maybe he was a creepy lolicon.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Sep 30, 2014 1:43 pm ElMariachi wrote: Maybe he tried to date her just because she was the famous European Eva Pilot? Or the standards about accepted age differences in the post-2I greatly changed.

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Nov 06, 2015 8:01 pm It seems that the Eva Shinkansen train has brought us official art of the sisters: Official Eva Store twitter is the source: https://twitter.com/eva_tokyo01/status/ ... 9565914112

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Nov 06, 2015 8:03 pm They're so cute!

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Nov 06, 2015 8:10 pm Must. Not. Let. Cuteness. Break. My. Hold! KAWAAAAAAAI!! Now. . . somebody do a time skip version of these adorable little girls. .

[]:             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Fri Nov 06, 2015 8:13 pm Wait, is Nozomi around the same age as Sakura? Also her purse is the train.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Nov 06, 2015 8:13 pm Nozomi is adorable. Kodama is . . . disappointing. I'm not into the whole glasses thing, but even apart from that she doesn't look related to the other two. It's cool to have official art for them, though. I hope some of that stuff shows up at Otakon next year.
